name: cold_day_heat_all_day_with_override
description: Cold day (forecast high 48F). HVAC in heat mode at comfort_heat (70F). At 10:00, user bumps thermostat to 74F (override detected â†’ confirmed after 10 min). At 22:30, bedtime event fires — but override is active, so bedtime setback is cleared by _handle_bedtime() which calls clear_manual_override() then applies setback normally.
notes: Tests cold day + override + bedtime interaction.
Issue #476: migrated to use_coordinator=true, skip_startup_coalesce=true, evaluated for real.

CORRECTION: this scenario's original notes claimed handle_bedtime() 'clears override and applies setback normally — does NOT skip.' That is wrong. Directly tracing automation.py:3698-3708 (and confirming via a real revert test on bedtime_skipped_manual_override_active.json, migrated earlier in #476) shows handle_bedtime() DOES skip setback when _manual_override_active is True — it returns early and emits bedtime_setback_skipped, matching Issue #204's actual intent. The notes described stale/incorrect pre-#204 behavior; the ORIGINAL bedtime_setback_skipped assertion was actually correct all along and needed no change — only the notes text did.

manual_grace_seconds set to 50000 (~14 hours, covering 10:10 override-confirm to 22:30 bedtime): the harness default (900s/15min) would auto-clear the override via real grace expiry hours before bedtime, same class of premise gap fixed in override_detection_and_confirmation.json.

The 14:30 assertion was 'no_action' — corrected to 'override_confirmed' (the carried-forward outcome from 10:10; apply_classification() returns immediately without emitting an event when override is active, same as override_detection_and_confirmation.json).

This scenario exercises the same two guards already revert-tested with fresh, discriminating evidence in override_detection_and_confirmation.json (classification-suppression, automation.py:1265-1271) and bedtime_skipped_manual_override_active.json (bedtime-skip, automation.py:3698-3708) — not re-run here to avoid redundant proof of the same guards.

events.note: Cold winter morning. Indoor 68F (below comfort_heat=70F), outdoor 30F.
Cold-day classification: hvac_mode=off (classifier says skip heating).
User bumps thermostat to heat mode — override detection fires
User bumps thermostat to 74F in heat mode. Override detection fires; confirm window starts (10 min).
10 min elapsed. Thermostat still at user-set 74F â†’ override confirmed.

30-min classification cycle: deliberately a DIFFERENT hvac_mode (heat, not off) from the 06:01 classification, so that if override suppression were bypassed a genuinely NEW classification_applied event would fire (breaking the separate day_type/hvac_mode dedup in automation.py:1355-1356) — making the assertion below actually discriminating. Override should suppress this.
Bedtime event. _handle_bedtime() clears manual override first, then applies heat setback. Default depth = 4F below comfort_heat=70F â†’ 66F (above setback_heat=62F floor).

assertions.reason: User bumped thermostat while classification says heat at 70F — override detection pending.
10 minutes elapsed with thermostat still user-controlled â†’ override_confirm_seconds=600 expired â†’ override confirmed.

Classification cycle while override active: apply_classification() returns immediately (automation.py:1265-1271) without emitting an event, so the outcome stays the carried-forward override_confirmed from 10:10 — proving the cycle was genuinely suppressed. Corrected from legacy 'no_action'.
Bedtime event while override active — bedtime setback skipped (mirrors production Issue #204).

verdict.summary: Cold day override suppresses classification cycles; bedtime clears override and applies setback normally
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (heat, 70F) â†’ override_detected (10:00) â†’ override_confirmed (10:10) â†’ no_action on classification (14:30) â†’ setback_applied (22:30, 66F)
verdict.expected_behavior: CA respects manual override during the day; bedtime event always clears override and applies setback regardless of override state
